dealKiller: Salfo is ~$35M revenue with active next-gen succession (Panagiotis Foteinos as CEO, Sofia Foteinou as Deputy CEO since 2024) — NOT a €4-7M succession-exposed founder shop. The headline target is mis-scoped by ~5x revenue and succession is already in motion internally, removing the founder-anxiety unlock. If the largest named target is wrong on size and succession, the rest of the target list needs re-verification before any €30K boutique mandate spend.
patternMatches: anchor-target misdescription (Salfo $35M rev, internal successor in place)

regulatory-tailwind overclaim (AKAEM/BIM mandate not yet binding — EU BIM Task Group + research confirms Greek BIM provisions remain 'without practical implications'; no enforceable 2025-26 public-tender requirement found)

AI-tool maturity overclaim (Forma BIM-element generation announced AU 2025, still 'driving AI upstream' — conceptual/schematic phase only; no production EU sub-€10M studio case study; quality threshold for Greek public deliverables unverified)

tailwind-duration overclaim (RRF hard-stops Aug 2026; Greece at 53% milestones Feb 2026 — backlog burns 18-24mo not 5yr; IOBE warns GDP growth drops to ~1% post-RRF; ESPA 2021-27 tail real but smaller and not A&E-concentrated)

labor-compression unsupported by evidence (no documented Greek/EU sub-€10M A&E P&L showing 70% CAD/BIM headcount cut without quality regression; BLS + ArchDaily data shows role-transformation not replacement; only ~6% architects routinely use AI)
valuation-bracket optimism (global A/E median 5.5x EBITDA, top-quartile 7.5x; Greek small-cap with succession-risk discount could land 4-6x BUT only if founder-exit, not internal-successor scenarios)

PE-can't-do-this hand-wave (Greek PE Diorama/EOS explicitly cover services/digitalization; absence of Greek A&E roll-up is information, not opportunity — likely signals labor-cut political risk under Law 4808/2021 collective-redundancy consultation)
unitEconomicsConcern: Earn-out structure is self-contradictory: 24mo earn-out tied to RRF backlog burn-down requires owners to deliver revenue through Aug 2026 hard-stop, while AI thesis demands 60%+ headcount cut Year 1. Greek collective-redundancy rules (Law 4808/2021) require union consultation and capped monthly dismissals; severance is multi-month with judicial-discretion uplift. Cutting 14 of 28 in Year 1 on a politically-visible founder-led shop is operationally and reputationally hostile — founders will resist and tender-quality reputation will crater mid-earn-out. The 32-37% EBITDA target post-overhaul assumes both labor cuts AND revenue uplift; you get one or the other, not both, in the earn-out window.
loadBearingAssumption: Three load-bearing assumptions all simultaneously required: (a) AKAEM/BIM compliance creates binding 2025-26 procurement requirement small studios can't meet — UNVERIFIED, evidence points to non-binding directive; (b) text-to-BIM tools produce production-quality output passing Greek public-tender stamping engineer review — UNVERIFIED, tools still in conceptual phase; (c) Greek tender-writing LLM output meets legal/procurement language threshold for €5-30M projects — UNVERIFIED, DIAVGEIA NLP research is information-extraction not generation. If any one fails, the AI margin uplift collapses to ~22-25% EBITDA, not 32-37%, and the 4-6x entry multiple becomes a fair-price-for-static-EBITDA acquisition with no alpha.
alternativeThesis: Reframe as 'AE-Services-Overlay' not 'AE-RollUp': do NOT acquire — instead launch a Greek-language AI-BIM/AI-tender-writing tooling+services overlay sold INTO sub-€5M Greek studios as a 30-40% gross-margin SaaS+services play, anchored on ELPEN captive demand and Tryfon family Min. of Health/Development relationships for one lighthouse public-sector reference. Cost €300-500K to build vs €10-15M to acquire 3-4 studios + integrate + restructure. Phase 0: paid LOI from 2 named studios for a €25-50K AI-BIM/tender-writing pilot in 12 weeks. If pilots show measurable productivity (not just demo), then acquisition optionality re-opens in 2027 at distressed multiples once RRF backlog drains and the sub-scale studios face real margin compression. The acquisition thesis is right about who's stressed and what tools help; it's wrong about the timing (too early — RRF still flush), the unlock (succession is partial not universal), and the structure (services > acquisition until tool maturity is proven in Greek context).
